name: Eekso — The Ritual (Boss)
dmNotes:
THE CLIMAX. The whole dungeon has been plumbing; this is the drain. Everything they've seen — tilted floors, warm pipes, dated sacks, the tended Torm — resolves here. Give them a beat to put it together before he speaks.

HIS CORE: devout, not gleeful. He took self-sacrifice one step too far: if giving your own flesh is holy, having MORE flesh to give is holier — other people are supply. He cuts himself constantly and heals with what he takes so he can cut again. He believes he is the most generous man alive. Gentle, tired, certain.

THE IMPOSTOR is background only — one beat of foreshadowing, never explained.

STAT BLOCKS: 📖 'The Necromancer of Eekso' (CR 8) + 'Flesh Offering' (CR 4) — campaign homebrew, 🏠 in the compendium. He + two offerings over the fight ≈ 6,100 XP, a proper climax.
⚑ HE HAS NO NAME YET — that's yours.
scriptText:
# The Ritual

## The Approach

- A wide, low hall — almost empty after the gallery

- The pipes came down with you: knee height, converging, and now you can hear what's in them clearly — slow, thick, unhurried. Like something swallowing, taking its time.

- Centre of the floor: a **great ring of stone**, two storeys high, sealed. Iron gates at either end, shut. The pipes run under its base.

- Its outer wall is crusted with **red crystal** — deep red shot with pink, in sprays and blooms like frost. Warm. Faintly luminous.

> [!dm] Let them circle it. The room outside the ring is safe on purpose — the last quiet moment.
>
> - **Arcana DC 15:** the crystal isn't mineral. It grew — out of something recently alive.
> - **Perception DC 12:** through the gate bars, something inside is moving *very slowly*
> - The southeast gate isn't locked. If they hesitate long enough, **it opens by itself.**

## The Gates

{{music:gates}}

- The smell comes out first: hot copper and rot, and under it something almost sweet — incense over a butcher's yard

- One enormous circular chamber, floored in dark slate

- The floor is cut with a lattice of **shallow channels**, spreading from the centre like the veins of a leaf

- The channels are full, and they are **running**

## The Wall

{{music:wall}} {{amb:fap-cave-whispers|vol=30%|in=6s}}

Around the inside of the chamber, ten feet in from the stone, there is a wall that is not made of stone.

It stands ten feet high, it goes the whole way around, and it is made of blood and flesh — held up by nothing, touching nothing. It moves the way deep water moves. Things surface in it and go under again. A hand. The curve of a shoulder. A face, briefly, mouth open, and then gone.

The pipes come up through the floor all around the chamber, and they are feeding it.

> [!dm] Read slowly. No adjectives after 'a face, briefly, mouth open, and then gone' — the silence does more.
>
> - **Arcana DC 16:** the wall is being *maintained* — not a spell that was cast, a spell being **held**, right now, continuously
> - Everything upstairs — the stacks, the mill, the gutters — *arrived here*

## The Symbol

- Where all the channels meet: a symbol **thirty feet across**

- The gauntlet of **Torm**, drawn in blood, raised with ground bone — so fresh in places it's still running into the grooves cut to carry it

- Somebody has kept it fresh for a very long time

> [!dm] **Religion DC 10** — anyone who saw the statue upstairs needs no roll.
>
> The two rooms rhyme: a statue kept clean with a rag, and this. Same devotion. Same hands.
>
> This is the moment the dungeon means. A beat of silence before he speaks.

## Him

{{music:him}}

He's kneeling at the edge of the symbol with his back to you, and he doesn't hurry. An ordinary-looking man in a plain robe, sleeves rolled. Both forearms are a mess of scars — hundreds, layered, some silver with age and some from this morning. A small clean knife on the stone by his knee.

He finishes what he's doing — drawing a line of his own blood along a channel with two fingers, carefully, the way you'd ice a cake — sits back on his heels, looks at you over his shoulder, and seems *pleased*.

> [!dm] Gentle. Tired. Certain. Not performing. Quiet delivery, real pauses.

**"Oh — you came all the way down. Most don't."**

He wipes his fingers on his robe and stands, and there's no threat in it at all.

**"Do you know what he asks for? Torm. Everyone thinks it's dying for someone. It isn't. Dying's easy — it's one afternoon, and then you're finished, and you've nothing left to give anybody.**

**"What he asks is that you keep giving. Every day. Of yourself. Until there's nothing left of you."**

He holds up his forearms and turns them over, like a man showing off a garden.

**"I ran out of myself in the second year. I was thirty-one. I had nothing left to offer and years and years still to give — do you understand what a *cruelty* that is? To be willing, and empty?"**

He smiles — the smile of somebody who solved a problem.

**"And then I was shown. If the giving is what's holy — then it doesn't matter whose. It only matters that it's given. And there is so *much* of it walking around out there, going to waste, carried about by people who'll never give a drop of it to anyone."**

He gestures, almost apologetically, at the wall of red behind him.

**"They're all still giving. Every one of them. I've kept them at it for years — do you know how hard that is? How much care that takes? I don't waste anybody."**

A beat. He looks at them properly for the first time, and something moves behind his eyes that isn't his.

**"You've all got so much. You've no idea what you're carrying."**

> [!dm] **Improv handles:**
>
> - *"Who showed you?"* → he opens his mouth, stops, genuinely confused for one second — reaching for a word he's always known. Then smiles and moves on. **The impostor. One beat. Don't explain.**
> - *"You're insane / this is evil"* → not offended — *sad for them*. "You'd let it all rot in the ground. And you think I'm the cruel one."
> - *"We're going to stop you"* → "Yes. Probably. It's alright — it'll all still be given."
> - *The people upstairs still breathing* → he **brightens**. Proud. Explains the technique. Worst thing he says.
> - **He does not attack first.** He'd rather they understood. When they move, he sighs — and picks up the knife.

## The Fight

{{music:boss}}

> [!dm] 📖 **Stat blocks: 'The Necromancer of Eekso' + 'Flesh Offering'** — campaign homebrew, 🏠 in the compendium. The clock IS the fight:
>
> - **He pays in his own flesh.** Every summon costs him hit points — he opens his arm and something stands up out of the channels. His self-sacrifice IS the threat: damaging him and racing his summons are the same clock.
> - **The wall heals him** every round while it stands and the pipes run. The party's lever: attack the pipes or the wall to shut it off. **Reward that fast.**
> - **Summons:** one big flesh thing first (Large, slow, grabby, hits hard); a second if the fight runs long. They rise out of the blood-channels, not from nowhere.
> - **Budget:** ~6,000–6,700 XP vs 4x lvl 6 + Cumb — necromancer ~CR 8 plus two CR 3–4 summons lands there
>
> {{music:summon}} **When the first one stands up, cut the talking.** A full round of nobody understanding what they're looking at.
>
> **Endgame:** when he drops, the wall loses what was holding it — it comes down **all at once**. Thirty feet of standing blood hitting stone. Everything in the room is soaked. Nobody gets clean.

## After

{{music:after}}

> [!dm] Land it quietly. No fanfare.
>
> - The channels stop running. The room goes quiet in a way it hasn't been in years.
> - Upstairs in the stacks, the ones still breathing stop. They die peacefully — the only ones here who do.
> - The knife is ordinary. Cheap, worn thin from sharpening. He'd had it a long time.
> - **His forearms:** the oldest scars are neat and evenly spaced. The recent ones are not. Somewhere in the middle, the handwriting changed.
>
> ⚑ **Threads for you:** what the impostor wanted, whether the wall was building toward something, whether any of it connects to the pool. All open — nothing forces an answer tonight.

ideas.text: If they hesitate at the gate too long, it opens by itself
Arcana: the wall isn't a spell that was cast, it's a spell being HELD, continuously, right now
He draws his own blood along the channel with two fingers, carefully, like icing a cake
'I ran out of myself in the second year. I was thirty-one.'
Ask who showed him and he blanks for a second — reaching for a word he's always known. The impostor. One beat only.
The oldest scars on his arms are neat and evenly spaced; the recent ones aren't. Somewhere in the middle the handwriting changed.
When he dies the wall drops all at once — thirty feet of standing blood hitting stone. Nobody gets clean.
If asked about the people upstairs still breathing, he brightens and explains the technique. Worst thing he says.

entities.name: The necromancer ⚑ unnamed
entities.note: 📖 homebrew 'The Necromancer of Eekso' (CR 8). Devout, gentle, certain. Pays 25 HP per summon; the five feed-pipes heal him — destroying them is the lever.

entities.name: The wall of blood and flesh
entities.note: Ten feet high, hovering off the stone, fed by the pipes. Heals him each round — destroying it is the party's lever.

entities.name: The gauntlet of Torm, in blood and bone
entities.note: Thirty feet across, kept fresh. Rhymes with the tended statue in the foyer.

entities.name: Flesh Offering
entities.note: 📖 homebrew 'Flesh Offering' (CR 4). Large, slow, grabby, 10-ft reach, two grapples. Rises out of the blood-channels; max two at once.

entities.name: The impostor's hand
entities.note: One beat of confusion when asked who showed him. Do not explain.
